steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The relative clause reads "which typically are woven from sumac... and ______ bold colors." The blank verb is joined by "and" to "are woven," so it must be parallel: same subject ("baskets" — plural), same tense (present), and same form as the main verb in "are woven."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'had featured': Past perfect tense. Shifts to the past and adds an unnecessary perfect aspect, breaking parallelism with present-tense "are woven." ✗
  • Choice B — 'were featuring': Past progressive tense. Shifts to the past and implies an action in progress, neither of which fits the general present-tense description of Sally Black's baskets. ✗
  • Choice C — 'featured': Simple past tense. The passage describes a current, general truth about the baskets, not a past event. Past tense breaks parallelism with "are woven." ✗
  • Choice D — 'feature': Simple present, plural — matches "baskets" (plural subject) and is parallel to "are woven." The sentence reads: "baskets, which typically are woven from sumac... and feature bold colors" — grammatically consistent. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
